name: Kabuverdianu (Cape Verdean Creole)

ancestors.notes: European Portuguese superstrate: colonial contact from 1460s. The lexifier. Vocabulary, much of morphology and some phonology derive from 15th–17th century Portuguese.

notes: Kabuverdianu / Cape Verdean Creole (ALUPEC orthography). Portuguese-based creole; vernacular language of Cape Verde (~600,000 speakers). Two macro-dialects: Badiu (Santiago island, Sotavento group) and Sampadjudo (Barlavento islands). KEY FEATURES: (1) NASAL VOWELS: phonemic; in ALUPEC written vowel+n (bon /bõ/), the coda n marking nasality and left unpronounced — the tilde is reserved for the diphthongs ãi/ãu (mãi, nãu). Handled here by the KEA_NASAL_* allophone rules. (2) FIVE-VOWEL SYSTEM: no /ɨ/; unstressed vowels preserved. (3) SILENT H: <h> has no phonetic value. (4) AFFRICATES: tx=[tʃ], dj=[dʒ] — innovations relative to PT. (5) WORD-FINAL WEAKENING: final obstruents may be deleted, especially in Badiu variety. (6) SIBILANT: x=[ʃ], j=[ʒ], s=[s]. (7) RHOTIC: single intervocalic r is a tap /ɾ/; the strong rhotic /ʀ/ is written rr and word-initial r. Official orthography ALUPEC (Alfabeto Unificado para a Escrita do Cabo-Verdiano) adopted 1998, revised 2009.

sources.author: Baptista, M.
sources.title: The Syntax of Cape Verdean Creole: The Sotavento Varieties
sources.notes: Definitive syntactic description; phonological inventory in chapter 2

sources.author: Veiga, M.
sources.title: O Cabo-verdiano em 45 Lições
sources.notes: Pedagogical description with phonological chapter; Barlavento variety focus

sources.author: Lang, J.
sources.title: Creole Genesis and the Acquisition of Grammar: The Case of Cape Verde Creole

allophone_rules.notes: ALUPEC writes nasal vowels as vowel+n: a vowel nasalises before a CODA nasal (bon /bõ/, kansa /ˈkãsa/); an onset nasal before a vowel (mininu, nha) stays oral. ALUPEC alphabet documentation; Baptista 2002, The Syntax of Cape Verdean Creole, ch. 2.
The coda nasal is absorbed into the nasal vowel it created; in ALUPEC the coda n marks nasality of the preceding vowel and is not itself pronounced.

stress.notes: Stress is penultimate by default; a written acute/circumflex marks a non-penultimate stressed vowel — karapáti 'tick' vs karapati 'to cling' (Baptista 2002; ALUPEC). Oral diphthongs are declared so the syllabifier counts each as one nucleus.

orthography_standard.name: ALUPEC — Alfabeto Unificado para a Escrita da Língua Cabo-verdiana
orthography_standard.notes: Approved on a five-year trial by Decreto-Lei 67/98 (1998) and instituted as the official alphabet by Decreto-Lei 8/2009. A phonemic, near-biunique grapheme–phoneme design, NOT the etymological Portuguese-based spelling. This spec targets ALUPEC: nasal vowels written vowel+n (the coda n unpronounced), tilde kept only for the diphthongs ãi/ãu; silent h; affricates tx /tʃ/ and dj /dʒ/; strong rhotic rr and word-initial r, single intervocalic r a tap.
